In this world is better to be dead or blind, some times you get to be both
Pale ones can rip a men apart in a matter of seconds with it's powerful claw shaped tentacles
The woman in the wasteland have been surviving fighting the High Alliance and the hordes of paleones since the great war ended.  Deadly and beautiful
Searching for resources in the wasteland is a safer task in greater numbers. But only if you face other people. If you are against a pale one, pray you are faster than your teammates
Some places are close for a very important reason.
In the wasteland people only can survive on the top of tall building, anyone on the ground is prey of cannibals and paleones
The High Alliance controls most of the tall building on the wasteland, and have a large and well equipped army that they use to rule with iron fist.

Rubber Ducky!  He's cute, he's squishy, and he's got a Rainbow Gun!  Rubber Ducky is square, yes, but does that mean that he has square shoes?  Does he have a square car?  Does he eat square cereal?  Yes, he may be square, but does a cat have cat-shaped shoes?  Do cartons of milk drive cars that take a rectangular shape?  Does the very grass outside your window eat grass-like cereal?  Questions to ponder indeed. . .

Rubber Fun Fact:
Rubber Ducky's sprite started out as the wall sprite in the game.

descriptions.name: Rubber Ducky (Trading Card)
descriptions.type: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun

descriptions.value:
These penguins know how to party, what with their affinity for pianos and teleportation abilities.  I do wonder, though: are they made to be square because Rubber Ducky is square, or was Rubber Ducky made to be square after the developer saw the square penguins?

Rubber Fun Fact:
The penguin "squeak" is actually a modified version of the sound made by a guitar when the strings are stroked with a pick.

descriptions.name: Teleporting Penguins
descriptions.type: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun

descriptions.value:
These Lizards tend to float up and down depending on the situation at hand.  They're know to be very prudent, which means they often have good advice for complicated situations!  And the bananas!  The Lizards always keep a stock-pile of Exploding Bananas around to give out to passer-bys as a gift.  These bananas are fairly popular among the tourist crowd, seeing as a "BOOM: BANANA" sort of a party lends itself to a good laugh and large-scale nutrition.

Rubber Fun Fact:
All animals in Rubber Ducky have eyes that don't fall into a direct line to give the idea that they're head is tilted, which the developer finds cute in real-life animals.

descriptions.name: Floating Lizards
descriptions.type: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun

descriptions.value:
Sentient accordions!  These guys are great street musicians!  Like, listen to those sweet, sweet, aerophone tones!  These guys can start up successful bands anywhere they go.  Now they just need a dance routine. . .

Rubber Fun Fact:
Every background in Rubber Ducky (except Digital Place and a secret level) have settings that move in time to the music.  Since there are so many particles and slowdown is rampant, the backgrounds had to be programmed to run according to the computer's inner clock instead of running with the rest of the game.

descriptions.name: The Accordions
descriptions.type: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun Trading Card

tags.localized_tag_name: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun

descriptions.value:
Rubber Ducky sees the Bug Brigade every once in a while.  They tend to always be on the move, which is really what they like.  The Bug Brigade, however, likes to be zapped with Rainbow Guns just as much as Spikies, so sometimes they'll make sure to stop by a place where someone has a Rainbow Gun to take a break.

Rubber Fun Fact:
Rubber Ducky saw a first try at many things for the developer, including voice acting, music composition, art, and a story-based game!

descriptions.name: Bug Brigade
descriptions.type: Rubber Ducky and the Rainbow Gun Trading Card

War on the frontier is desperate.  Many women have been called into service by both sides in the war between the Federation and the Alliance.
War on the frontier is desperate.  Many men have been called into service by both sides in the war between the Federation and the Alliance.
Working in secret against the Federation on the frontier.  What are her motives?

The pride of the Federation, the Manta class heavy fighter rules the skies.  The Manta sports the agility of a lighter fighter class while still bringing to bear superior firepower and shields.  Working alongside the lighter Saber fighters, the Manta enforces the will of the Federation in the core worlds and out on the Frontier.

The Federation Corvette is a deadly mid-sized ship, and is a mainstay of the larger Federation fleet.  The Corvette is almost able to match fighter speeds, while bringing the weapons and armor of a superior class of ship.  Well balanced and deadly, the Corvette excels against fighter packs or against capital ships.

The Alliance Gunship is a heavy fighter which has been in production for many years.  The basic design is an update to the outdated Gemini Fighter.  Gunships can be found in local militias and some pirate units out of the alliance.  The Gunship is slower than most fighters, but packs unrivaled weapons, armor and shields.

The Alliance Stingray is a scrap built torpedo platform.  Nicknamed the "Junk Fighter" by Federation pilots, the Stingray does not have any of the latest technology.  Little more than cargo and civilian shuttles loaded with missiles, the Stingray is still deadly to large installations.
The Vor Dragon is one of the larger, more aggressive Vor species.  Able to fire sticky plasma balls that disable or destroy it's prey they should be approached with caution.

The Pirate Interceptor is the last thing a civilian trading vessel wants to see.  These light fighters are cheap to produce.  Focusing on top speed, agility and weaponry, the Interceptor is deadly in the hands of a good pilot.  Interceptors have paper thin armor and weak shields.  Their speed is their defense.

The Pirate Smuggler is an all purpose cargo vessel.  Retrofitted with additional guns and sensors the smuggler is ideal for moving contraband in the Frontier.  A mid sized ship it is more than a match for a small fighter wing or unprepared cargo ship.
The basic gameplay is move and shoot.  Its that simple.
Sometimes you will have to fight off waves of enemies in a tight space.
The more fire power you have the faster you can clear waves of enemies.
Avoiding getting hit is the best thing you could do to avoid taking damage.
Drones are a very helpful tool in helping you survive and clear waves of enemies.
There are projectiles that can pass through enemies and walls.  These are very useful.
The best place to fight off waves of enemies would be an open arena.

Many Gladiators come from the ranks of the military, such as Centurions.
This is the oldest arena in the kingdom, located in Pompeii, now long since abandoned and crumbling. Many warriors gave their lives in this coliseum, earning it the name 'Killing Grounds'.
A Gladiator's first match can be eye-opening and very instructional. This warrior is about to learn the meaning of pain -- and sacrifice.
The experienced Gladiator uses their pain to fuel their anger and strength, making them unstoppable.
One of the largest arenas in existence and is the site for most championship matches. The sky is said to be red partly due to the blood spilled here.
The largest graveyard in the kingdom is home to many fallen Gladiators. It is said the ghosts of these warriors haunt the grounds to this day.
